# Information Security Policy

Owner: Mikhail Kurzin
Area: InfoSec
Last edited time: June 10, 2025
Approvers: Mike Yan, Dmitry Kushnikov, Antony Gorin
Document ID: POL-IS-01
Document Type: Policy
Document application: All Contractors, All Employees
Effective date: December 12, 2022
Status: Published
Tag: ISO27001
Version: 3

# Table of Contents

---

# **1. General**

## **1.1 Purpose**

The document describes the Company's approach for information security across Manychat to ensure customer data is protected from unauthorized access, disclosure, modification, or destruction (deliberate or accidental) throughout the information lifecycle.

This document outlines the security best practices and frameworks upon which the Manychat platform is developed and maintained to keep customer’s data and internal confidential data safe.

## **1.2 Scope**

This policy applies to all divisions, teams, and services of Manychat and all personnel of Manychat. It also applies to external organizations and their personnel who have been granted access to the Manychat infrastructure and services.

This policy shall be provided to Manychat customers and relevant interested parties, as appropriate. Manychat employees are made aware of the contents of this policy and their commitment.

The document is reviewed at least annually to ensure it meets the requirements of Manychat partners, customers, related government organizations, as well as Manychat business objectives.

This Policy is the top-level document. Additional standards, procedures and instructions have been developed to detail information security processes. This is done in accordance with the documented information hierarchy below.

![Screenshot 2025-12-04 at 12.42.21 PM.png](Information%20Security%20Policy/Screenshot_2025-12-04_at_12.42.21_PM.png)

[](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

# **2. Policy**

## **2.2 Summary**

**Manychat** is a marketing platform for businesses to connect with customers and potential customers.

**Manychat's mission** is to help businesses grow by building meaningful relationships with their customers.

**Manychat** is committed to provide a safe and secure environment to its customers and would like to give assurance to all stakeholders that the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data are adequately maintained.

**Manychat's strategic goal** is to support the growth of the Company by developing cyber resilience and building the trust of partners and customers. Towards that goal Manychat has established an **Information Security Management System (ISMS**) that is based on the ISO/IEC 27001:2022 framework. The Company believes that establishing, implementing, maintaining, and continually improving the ISMS helps to combat cybersecurity threats.

**Manychat** is committed to meet all applicable Information Security requirements within this Policy from the customers, to satisfy applicable expectations from the interested parties, and to provide necessary resources to achieve these goals, as well as to regularly review information security performance in order to ensure that it improves over time, consequently continually improves ISMS and stays compliant with applicable legal and other obligations.

**Leadership team** has the overall responsibility for managing the Company’s values effectively and satisfactorily according to current laws, requirements, and contracts.

## **2.2 Principles**

**For the implementation of information security, Manychat has defined the following Information Security Principles:**

- Providing a high level of security, reliability, and availability of services is an important business factor;
- Manychat aims to establish itself as a provider of highly secure and reliable services;
- Security is smoothly integrated into business processes within Manychat to ensure that a high level of security is maintained even in a changing environment;
- Trust in the effectiveness of the security measures is achieved through an independent audit and certification based on established assessment criteria;
- Security is proactive in its approach and monitors security situations in the industry and globally.

## **2.3 Goals**

**The following goals outline the framework for the implementation of information security principles within Manychat.**

**The goals for information security are:**

- Manage information security risks in order to prevent or reduce undesired business effects;
- Integrate risk assessment and appropriate security controls into the business processes including the development of Manychat platform;
- Obtain a clear view of the assets that need to be protected and their criticality;
- Protect assets against any form of misuse, damage or loss;
- Protect the Company’s reputation in the information sphere;
- Ensure availability and reliability of IT infrastructure and services supplied and operated by Manychat;
- Continuously monitor the security situation allowing critical situations to be detected in advance and the impact of security critical events to be limited;
- Ensure that the Company is capable to continue its operation even after major security incidents;
- Deliver a secure products to customers;
- Ensure the protection of personal data (privacy);
- Foster cyber awareness and culture;
- Ensuring the ISMS can achieve its intended outcomes;
- Continually Increasing cybersecurity maturity level.

## **2.4 Policy Statements**

The Information Security Policy is structured within eight main sections outlined below.

### 2.4.1 Asset management

Manychat takes reasonable actions to identify assets and their level of criticality. The full inventory and categorization are the basis to select and implement optimal technical and organizational security measures to make sure that the assets and information are protected.

An Information Asset is any information or information system which is sensitive, confidential, or has value to Manychat, including third-party information processed by Manychat.

The Manychat assets must be appropriately protected. All assets (data, information, software, computers, communications equipment, service utilities, and individuals) must be accounted for and have an owner. Owners should be identified for all assets and they will be responsible for the maintenance and protection of their assets.

Critical and sensitive information should be physically protected from unauthorized access, damage, and interference.

### 2.4.2 Risk Management

Risk Management is at the core of the ISMS to identify and estimate the magnitude of the risks.

Identification, assessment, and evaluation of risks are enabling effective management of information security vulnerabilities and threats to information assets that could adversely affect business or provide business opportunities.

Manychat utilizes an integrated risk management approach with focuses on both technical and operational security practices. Ongoing and systematic risk assessment is a consistent part of selecting appropriate improvement protection controls and ensuring that customer’s data is safe.

### 2.4.3 Access Management

Manychat invests a lot in the improvement of internal access controls to minimize the risks of incidents caused by access bypass by an internal or external intruder, including contractors with access to company assets.

The Company's biggest priority is to manage access based on business requirements and granted only with consideration of “Need to know” and “Least privilege” principles.

Access to business systems and information must be restricted to authorized personnel only, controlled by secret passwords or other security credentials assigned and traceable to individual employees.

Manychat must segregate the Company environments to make sure that customer’s data is protected from any kind of unauthorized access including test or analytics purposes usage.

Access for employees, partners, suppliers and other parties should be granted according to their role and only to a level that will allow them to carry out their duties.

### 2.4.4 Employee security and awareness

Manychat is focused on employee security awareness as a key driver to improve overall security maturity level and culture. This helps employees to understand what security threats are, the potential impact of security incidents and the steps required to reduce or prevent risks.

The Manychat security policies must be communicated to all employees, contractors, and third parties to ensure that they understand their responsibilities.

Security responsibilities must be included in job descriptions and in terms and conditions of employment.

Verification checks should be carried out on all new employees, contractors, and third parties.

### 2.4.5 Application and IT Infrastructure Security

Given the number of security threats that are known for the modern web applications, secure design, implementation, and monitoring are essential to stable business operations.

The Company makes information security an integral part of the Manychat platform throughout its lifecycle. The Company ensures that information security is addressed as part of the security practices including:

- **Segregation of environments**. Manychat segregates development and production environments to make sure that Data is protected from any kind of unauthorized access.
- **Encryption in transit.** All external network communications are protected with encryption. We support the latest recommended secure cipher suites to encrypt all traffic in transit, including use of TLS 1.2 protocols, AES256 encryption, and SHA2 hash functions, whenever supported by the clients.
- **Encryption at rest.** Customer data at rest is encrypted using FIPS 140-2 compliant encryption standards, which applies to all types of data at rest within Manychat’s systems—relational databases, file drives, backups, etc. Access to cryptographic keys is restricted to a limited number of authorized Manychat personnel.
- **Redundancy**. Manychat selects IT Infrastructure suppliers that are committed to provide mechanisms with build-in security best practices for confidentiality, integrity, and availability. Manychat’s main IaaS provider AWS (EU-CENTRAL Frankfurt, EU) is committed to meet strict Disaster Recovery (DR) Service Level Agreement. Data availability is supported by the system architecture design and data backups.
- **Vulnerability assessment**. Manychat performs automated and manual application and infrastructure security testing to identify and patch potential security vulnerabilities. Critical software patches are evaluated, tested, and applied proactively.
- **Penetration Testing.** We engage independent service providers to perform penetration tests to assess the potential system security threats at least on an annual basis.
- **Storage.** Manychat’s production databases and data processing servers are hosted in a data center located in AWS (Frankfurt, EU). Manychat maintains complete administrative control over the databases and virtual servers, and no third-party vendors have logical access to Personal Data.
- **Change Management.** Manychat implements documented change management procedures that provide a consistent approach for controlling, implementing, and documenting changes (including emergency changes) for Manychat’s software, information systems or network architecture.
- **Network security.** All network access between servers is restricted, using access control lists to allow only authorized services to interact in the network. We utilize third-party tools to detect, mitigate, and prevent Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) attacks.

Appropriate operating procedures should be put in place in order to achieve these security practices.

The information security requirements should be defined during the development of business requirements for new information systems or changes to existing information systems.

### 2.4.6 Supplier management

Manychat is selecting appropriate suppliers and partnerships in the hardening of data protection, including contract relations, to address secure data processing within a third-party environment.

Manychat ensures the protection of information assets whether they are accessible by suppliers.

The controls of suppliers should maintain agreed levels of information security and service delivery in line with the appropriate agreements/contracts.

### 2.4.7 Incident management

Manychat provides preventive, corrective, and detective measures, ensuring a consistent and effective approach to the management of information security incidents, including communication of events and weaknesses along with the appropriate follow-ups as well as if customers have been affected by the incident.

Manychat engages all employees, contractors, and customers to report on the alleged or known events and incidents related to information security, on observed or alleged deficiencies in the Manychat application and internal systems ([security@manychat.com](mailto:security@manychat.com)).

Manychat security team conducts investigations of information security incidents in accordance with the Company chain of custody.

Knowledge gained from analyzing and resolving information security incidents used to reduce the likelihood or impact of future incidents indicates the need for enhanced or additional controls and may be taken into account in the security policy review process and used in user awareness training due care of confidentiality aspects. Information security incidents and vulnerabilities associated with information systems should be communicated in a timely manner. Appropriate corrective action must be taken.

Formal incident reporting and escalation should be implemented.

All employees, contractors, and third-party users must be made aware of the procedures for reporting the different types of security incidents, or vulnerabilities that might have an impact on the security of Manychat’s assets.

Information security incidents and vulnerabilities must be reported as quickly as possible.

### 2.4.8 Monitoring and review

Manychat performs a variety of internal assessments and retrospective reviews to identify areas of improvement of applied security controls alongside the product functionality itself.

Manychat involves external independent reviewers to assess the adequacy of the amount of implemented technical measures (such as external penetration testing) and operational processes themselves.

Manychat continuously monitors assets and components against known threats and vulnerabilities. Together with the risk management approach Manychat continuously works on improvement of their timely discovery and remediation.

Monitoring fit-for-purpose and keeping pace with new threats, producing alerts for analysis, and identifying events for action and remediation in a timely manner commensurate to the risk.

Vulnerability management involves alerting and responding to identified and potential violations or security threats in a timely, measured, and prioritized (risk-based) manner, in order to prevent or limit the damage. Vulnerability management is considered a preventive and corrective measure.

# **3. Roles and Responsibilities**

**The CEO** has the overall responsibility for Security at the Company, including information security.

Specific roles and responsibilities:

| **Role** | **Role description** |
| --- | --- |
| **Head of Security** | Head of Security is responsible for the maintenance and review of the ISMS Policy, standards, processes, and procedures. |
| **All employees** | All staff and employees of Manychat, contractual third parties, and agents of the organization accessing the organization’s information are required to adhere to the ISMS Policy, processes, standards, and procedures. |
| **Line managers** | Line managers are responsible for ensuring that all staff and employees, contractual third parties, and agents of the organization are made aware of and comply with the ISMS Policy, processes, standards and procedures across their functions. |
| **Security team** | Security team is responsible for monitoring overall awareness of this Policy, investigation of the violations, and deviation handling. |
| **Internal auditors** | Internal auditors should review the adequacy of the controls that are implemented to protect Manychat’s information and recommend improvements where deficiencies are found. |

# **4. Compliance**

The proof of ISMS quality is the receipt and maintenance of the certificate of ISO/IEC 27001:2022 «Information Technology. Security techniques. Information security management systems. Requirements» compliance.

Manychat must comply with multiple state regulatory requirements of countries, Manychat operates in, such as:

- The General Data Protection Regulation GDPR (Regulation EU 2016/679);
- Personal data protection regulation in the countries or regions of operation (including CCPA and Law of Armenia on protection of personal data (Regulation ЗR-49-N));
- Canada’s Anti-Spam Law (or “CASL”);
- The CAN-SPAM Act;
- other mandatory state regulatory acts, including acts on commercial secrets and intellectual property.

## **4.1 Enforcement**

Implementation and enforcement of this policy is ultimately the responsibility of all employees at Manychat. Security team may conduct ad-hoc checks to ensure compliance with this document without notice. Any violation shall require immediate corrective action. Violations shall be noted in the Manychat applications and responsible employees shall be dispatched to remediate the issue.

For Manychat employees, breaches of this policy may result in disciplinary action being taken and will be dealt with under the disciplinary process in accordance with state laws and regulations of the residence country. Any serious breaches or repeated breaches of this policy may be deemed by Manychat as gross misconduct and may result in disciplinary sanctions, including a remark, reprimand, and dismissal from work.

Managers are responsible for ensuring their direct reports (whether those direct reports are permanent employees, contractors, sub-contractors, or agency personnel) are familiar with the requirements of this policy.
